description: record_vitals() builds the INSERT query with an f-string. patient_id and vital_type are injected directly into the SQL string without sanitisation. An attacker who controls patient_id can execute arbitrary SQL.
fix: Use parameterised queries: cursor.execute('INSERT INTO vitals (patient_id, vital_type, value) VALUES (%s, %s, %s)', (patient_id, vital_type, value))

description: DB_PASSWORD is hardcoded as a plaintext string. Any developer with read access to the repository has the production database password.
fix: Replace with os.getenv('DB_PASSWORD'). Rotate the exposed credential immediately.

description: get_patient_vitals() returns full_name, dob, and nhs_number in the query result. This PII is over-exposed to any caller of this function.
fix: Select only the fields needed: SELECT v.vital_type, v.value, v.ts FROM vitals v WHERE v.patient_id = %s

description: _fire_alert() has no rate limiting — fires on every reading that breaches threshold, including during a retry loop.
fix: Add a cooldown check: only fire an alert if no alert was fired for this patient+vital_type in the last 60 seconds.
